title: platform-user — authorization policies
description: Two policies. Both are gated EXCLUSIVELY behind the `platform_admin` guard — the tenant-audience `sanctum` guard NEVER interacts with PlatformUser or PlatformProfile rows. `PlatformUserPolicy` gates the CRUD + lifecycle transitions (view, hire, suspend, restore, offboard). `PlatformProfilePolicy` gates the PII satellite (self-service `me` endpoints + admin views). Every policy ability that mutates state OR unmutes PII writes a fresh audit row (meta-audit) with the caller's actor id + the specific ability invoked — SOC2 CC6.1 evidence for who saw what.

abilities.scope: Any Stackra staffer with the ops or product baseline role can list. Redacted list (email hash-fingerprint + display_name + department; no PII).
Same audience as viewAny. Redacted detail view. See `viewPii` and `viewHrContext` for the unmute abilities.
Security + HR only. Unmutes email + phone + Slack/GitHub handles on the response. Never unmutes credential fields (those live on Identity and are regulated_secret).

HR only. Unmutes offboarding_reason + suspension_reason (free-form justification text). Distinct from viewPii — HR context is separately gated because it may include HR-sensitive language beyond mere PII.

super_admin + hr only. Actor must hold this permission to invoke the `platform-user:hire` command or POST /platform-users. Enforces MFA-on-identity gate at policy-time (returns 403 with `IDENTITY_MFA_REQUIRED` if the target identity has no MFA).

super_admin can update any row. hr can update employment-metadata columns only (department, employment_type, job_title, manager_id, hire_date). Everyone else can update only their own PlatformProfile via the me endpoints.
Security + super_admin only. Requires a reason (min 20 chars).
Same actors as `suspend` — must be equal or higher privilege to reverse a suspend.
super_admin + hr only. Terminal. Requires reason (min 20 chars) + optional effective_at. Refuses when the target has direct reports (must be reassigned first).
Compliance + super_admin only. Returns a 90-day rolling window of audit + activity rows keyed by this PlatformUser as actor.
Every active PlatformUser — seeded on every platform_admin role by default. Reads own PlatformProfile row via /platform-profiles/me.

Every active PlatformUser writes own profile fields (display_name, avatar_url, phone_e164, slack_handle, github_handle, timezone). first_name / last_name are HR-controlled and NOT editable via self-service.
Security + HR only. Unmutes phone_e164, slack_handle, github_handle on other PlatformUsers' profiles.
The on-call escalation resolver requires unredacted phone_e164 for the resolved shift. This ability unmutes phone_e164 ONLY on the on-call endpoint (not the general viewPii).

boundary_note: These policies apply EXCLUSIVELY to the `platform_admin` guard. Under the `sanctum` guard, PlatformUser + PlatformProfile rows are never first-class resources — a tenant caller cannot list, show, or mutate them. Any tenant-audience code that reaches an authorization refusal here has taken a wrong path (it should be routed through `access/delegation` action DTOs instead). Guard boundary is enforced at the auth-service action layer, not by these policies.
least_privilege_note: New PlatformUser hires land with NO roles seeded (see permissions.json). The `platform.platform_profiles.view.self` + `.update.self` abilities are the ONLY ones that a freshly-hired PlatformUser holds — they can read + write their own profile only, until a super_admin grants additional roles. This is the CORE least-privilege posture of the platform plane.
